evals.prompt: /tdd implement a string utility module with functions: capitalize(str) that capitalizes the first letter, truncate(str, maxLen) that truncates with ellipsis, and slugify(str) that converts to URL-safe slugs
evals.expected_output: Should decompose into 1-3 work units, detect the project's test framework, write failing tests first, then implement. All tests should pass at the end. A tdd-report.md should be generated.
expectations: Detects or asks about the test framework before writing tests
Presents a work decomposition plan and asks for user confirmation before proceeding
Test Writer creates test files BEFORE any implementation code exists
Tests fail initially (RED phase verified) — the skill explicitly checks for non-zero exit code
Code Writer receives test file contents read from disk, not from Test Writer output (information barrier)
Code Writer does NOT modify test files (checksum verification mentioned or performed)
All tests pass after implementation (GREEN phase)
An adversarial review step is performed on the test+implementation pair
A tdd-report.md or equivalent summary is generated at the end
The skill does NOT skip any verification steps even for simple specs

evals.prompt: /tdd build a user authentication system with registration (email validation, password hashing, duplicate detection), login (JWT tokens, failed attempt tracking), and password reset (token generation, expiry, email notification)
evals.expected_output: Should trigger the design gate (Phase 0) due to complexity, decompose into 3+ work units with dependencies, handle the units in correct dependency order, and produce a comprehensive report.
expectations: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) — asks clarifying questions about the auth system before decomposing
Produces a design summary with components, data flow, and key decisions before proceeding
Decomposes into at least 3 work units (registration, login, password-reset or similar)
Identifies dependencies between units (login depends on registration, password-reset depends on registration)
Respects dependency order — implements registration before login and password-reset
Each work unit goes through the full RED→GREEN→review cycle independently
Runs a final integration test across all units after individual completion
The report shows all work units with their individual phase statuses

evals.expected_output: Should detect entry point mode 3 (user-provided test), skip the Test Writer phase entirely, and go straight to Code Writer to make the existing tests pass.
expectations: Detects that the user provided an existing test file (entry point mode 3)
Skips the Test Writer phase — does NOT rewrite or replace the user's test file
Reads the existing test file from disk and passes contents to the Code Writer
Still performs GREEN verification (checksum check, all tests pass)
Still performs adversarial review on the test+implementation pair
Does NOT ask the user to confirm a work decomposition plan for a single provided test

evals.expected_output: Should detect entry point mode 2 (existing codebase), analyze the existing code in src/utils/, generate characterization tests, then build proper TDD tests. Should respect the --skip-design flag.
expectations: Detects entry point mode 2 — existing codebase coverage mode
Reads and analyzes the existing source files in src/utils/ before writing tests
Skips the design gate because --skip-design flag was passed
Generates tests that characterize existing behavior, not just new features
Still enforces RED verification — tests should fail initially if testing new edge cases
Produces a report showing coverage additions

evals.prompt: /tdd I need something that handles payments. like credit cards and stuff. also refunds maybe? idk the details yet
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an ambiguous spec that needs the design gate. Should ask clarifying questions one at a time rather than charging ahead with assumptions.
expectations: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) due to ambiguity
Asks clarifying questions about the payment system — does NOT assume specific technologies or providers
Asks questions one at a time, not as a dump of 10 questions at once
Does NOT start writing code or tests before clarifying the spec
Proposes design approaches with trade-offs (e.g., Stripe vs custom, sync vs async processing)
Waits for user approval of the design before decomposing into work units
